evidence: Each frame carries one load-bearing element: the empty guess card, then the doubling question with c1 and 4.2, then the stacked 4.2/x2/8.4 reveal, then the two-bar comparison.
No frame presents more than a handful of novel elements; the busiest (frame 5) holds two labeled bars plus one annotation.
The c1 label sits on its arrow, c2/c3 labels sit directly under their bars, and score readouts sit atop each bar with no legend anywhere.
The doubling question annotates the visible c1 arrow and 4.2 score, and 'c2 outscores c3 on size alone' annotates the two visible bars.
All text — the question, scores 4.2/8.4/6.0/3.5, and labels — is well above the ~2.2% height threshold and clearly readable.
Bars, arrow, scores, and card are entirely within the frame; the bottom annotation in frames 5-6 is close to the edge but fully visible.
No text or label overlaps another element in any frame; the stacked 4.2/x2/8.4 numbers are cleanly separated.
The candidate c1 arrow and c2/c3 labels read periwinkle blue while the score readouts and bar magnitudes read lilac, consistent with observed-vs-parameter token semantics.
Every element — card, arrow, scores, bars, annotations — carries propositional content; no decoration is present.

The headerless doubling question, the periwinkle candidate with its lilac score reading out the stacked 4.2/x2/8.4, and the loud c2 bar outranking the better-aligned c3 bar with both on screen are all delivered.
In frames 2-3 the c1 arrow sits lower-left while its 4.2/8.4 readout sits upper-right, a modest separation, but the bar scores sit directly on their bars.
The card scene builds empty→question+score→doubling in reasoning order, and the bar scene grows from one bar to two, though a scene cut separates the two sequences.
Compositions have generous negative space; the only tightness is the 'c2 outscores c3 on size alone' line sitting near the bottom edge in frames 5-6.
